name: hivemind-bus-client

metadata.name: hivemind-bus-client
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Hivemind Websocket Client

![logo](./logo.png)

## Install

```bash
pip install hivemind_bus_client
```

## Usage

```python
from time import sleep
from ovos_bus_client import Message
from hivemind_bus_client import HiveMessageBusClient
from hivemind_bus_client.decorators import on_escalate, \
    on_shared_bus, on_ping, on_broadcast, on_propagate, on_mycroft_message, \
    on_registry_opcode, on_third_party, on_cascade, on_handshake, on_hello, \
    on_rendezvous, on_hive_message, on_third_party, on_payload

key = "super_secret_access_key"
crypto_key = "ivf1NQSkQNogWYyr"

bus = HiveMessageBusClient(key, crypto_key=crypto_key, ssl=False)

bus.run_in_thread()


@on_mycroft_message(payload_type="speak", bus=bus)
def on_speak(msg):
    print(msg.data["utterance"])


mycroft_msg = Message("recognizer_loop:utterance",
                      {"utterances": ["tell me a joke"]})
bus.emit_mycroft(mycroft_msg)


sleep(50)

bus.close()
```

## Cli Usage

```bash
$ hivemind-client --help
Usage: hivemind-client [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  escalate      escalate a single mycroft message
  propagate     propagate a single mycroft message
  send-mycroft  send a single mycroft message
  terminal      simple cli interface to inject utterances and print speech


$ hivemind-client set-identity --help
Usage: hivemind-client set-identity [OPTIONS]

  persist node identity / credentials

Options:
  --key TEXT       HiveMind access key
  --password TEXT  HiveMind password
  --siteid TEXT    location identifier for message.context
  --help           Show this message and exit.


$ hivemind-client terminal --help
Usage: hivemind-client terminal [OPTIONS]

  simple cli interface to inject utterances and print speech

Options:
  --key TEXT      HiveMind access key
  --host TEXT     HiveMind host
  --port INTEGER  HiveMind port number
  --help          Show this message and exit.


$ hivemind-client send-mycroft --help
Usage: hivemind-client send-mycroft [OPTIONS]

  send a single mycroft message

Options:
  --key TEXT      HiveMind access key
  --host TEXT     HiveMind host
  --port INTEGER  HiveMind port number
  --msg TEXT      ovos message type to inject
  --payload TEXT  ovos message json payload
  --help          Show this message and exit.


$ hivemind-client escalate --help
Usage: hivemind-client escalate [OPTIONS]

  escalate a single mycroft message

Options:
  --key TEXT      HiveMind access key
  --host TEXT     HiveMind host
  --port INTEGER  HiveMind port number
  --msg TEXT      ovos message type to inject
  --payload TEXT  ovos message json payload
  --help          Show this message and exit.


$ hivemind-client propagate --help
Usage: hivemind-client propagate [OPTIONS]

  propagate a single mycroft message

Options:
  --key TEXT      HiveMind access key
  --host TEXT     HiveMind host
  --port INTEGER  HiveMind port number
  --msg TEXT      ovos message type to inject
  --payload TEXT  ovos message json payload
  --help          Show this message and exit.

```

functions.name: hivemind_bus_client.util.payload2dict
documentation:
helper to ensure all subobjects of a payload are a dict safe for serialization
eg. ensure payload is valid to send over mycroft messagebus object

functions.name: hivemind_bus_client.util.get_payload
documentation:
helper to read normalized payload
from all supported formats (HiveMessage, Message, json str)

functions.name: hivemind_bus_client.util.get_hivemsg
documentation:
helper to create a normalized HiveMessage object
from all supported formats (Message, json str, dict)

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.HANDSHAKE
documentation: negotiate initial connection

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.BUS
documentation: request meant for internal mycroft-bus in master

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.SHARED_BUS
documentation: passive sharing of message

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.INTERCOM
documentation: from satellite to satellite

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.BROADCAST
documentation: forward message to all slaves

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.PROPAGATE
documentation: forward message to all slaves and masters

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.ESCALATE
documentation: forward message up the authority chain to all

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.HELLO
documentation: like escalate, used to announce the device

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.QUERY
documentation: like escalate, but stops once one of the nodes can

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.CASCADE
documentation: like propagate, but expects a response back from

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.PING
documentation: like cascade, but used to map the network

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.RENDEZVOUS
documentation: reserved for rendezvous-nodes

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.THIRDPRTY
documentation: user land message, do whatever you want

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMessageType.BINARY
documentation: binary data container, payload for something else

classes.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMindBinaryPayloadType

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMindBinaryPayloadType.UNDEFINED
documentation: no info provided about binary contents

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMindBinaryPayloadType.RAW_AUDIO
documentation: binary content is raw audio  (TODO spec exactly what "raw audio" means)

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMindBinaryPayloadType.NUMPY_IMAGE
documentation: binary content is an image as a numpy array, eg. webcam picture

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMindBinaryPayloadType.FILE
documentation: binary is a file to be saved, additional metadata provided elsewhere

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMindBinaryPayloadType.STT_AUDIO_TRANSCRIBE
documentation: full audio sentence to perform STT and return transcripts

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMindBinaryPayloadType.STT_AUDIO_HANDLE
documentation: full audio sentence to perform STT and handle transcription immediately

class_variables.name: hivemind_bus_client.message.HiveMindBinaryPayloadType.TTS_AUDIO
documentation: synthesized TTS audio to be played

Pseudo extension type for binary payloads
it doesnt describe the payload but rather provides instruction to hivemind about how to handle it

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.identity.NodeIdentity.name
documentation:
human readable label, not guaranteed unique
can describe functionality, brand, capabilities or something else...

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.identity.NodeIdentity.public_key
documentation: ASCI public PGP key

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.identity.NodeIdentity.private_key
documentation:
path to PRIVATE .asc PGP key, this cryptographic key
uniquely identifies this device across the hive and proves it's identity

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.identity.NodeIdentity.password
documentation:
password is used to generate a session aes key on handshake.
It should be used instead of users manually setting an encryption key.
This password can be thought as identifying a sub-hive where all devices
can connect to each other (access keys still need to be valid)

classes.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HiveMessageWaiter

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HiveMessageWaiter._handler
documentation: Receive response data.

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HiveMessageWaiter.wait
documentation:
Wait for message.

Arguments:
    timeout (int or float): seconds to wait for message

Returns:
    HiveMessage or None

Wait for a single message.

Encapsulate the wait for a message logic separating the setup from
the actual waiting act so the waiting can be setuo, actions can be
performed and _then_ the message can be waited for.

Arguments:
    bus: Bus to check for messages on
    message_type: message type to wait for

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HivePayloadWaiter._handler
documentation: Receive response data.

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HiveMessageBusClient.on_open
documentation:
Handle the "open" event from the websocket.
A Basic message with the name "open" is forwarded to the emitter.

documentation: event handlers

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HiveMessageBusClient.on
documentation: event api

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HiveMessageBusClient.wait_for_message
documentation:
Wait for a message of a specific type.

Arguments:
    message_type (HiveMessageType): the message type of the expected message
    timeout: seconds to wait before timeout, defaults to 3

Returns:
    The received message or None if the response timed out
utility

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HiveMessageBusClient.wait_for_payload
documentation:
Wait for a message of a specific type + payload of a specific type.

Arguments:
    payload_type (str): the message type of the expected payload
    message_type (HiveMessageType): the message type of the expected message
    timeout: seconds to wait before timeout, defaults to 3

Returns:
    The received message or None if the response timed out

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HiveMessageBusClient.wait_for_response
documentation:
Send a message and wait for a response.

Arguments:
    message (HiveMessage): message to send, mycroft Message objects also accepted
    reply_type (HiveMessageType): the message type of the expected reply.
                                  Defaults to "<message.msg_type>".
    timeout: seconds to wait before timeout, defaults to 3

Returns:
    The received message or None if the response timed out

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.client.HiveMessageBusClient.wait_for_payload_response
documentation:
Send a message and wait for a response.

Arguments:
    message (HiveMessage): message to send, mycroft Message objects also accepted
    payload_type (str): the message type of the expected payload
    reply_type (HiveMessageType): the message type of the expected reply.
                                  Defaults to "<message.msg_type>".
    timeout: seconds to wait before timeout, defaults to 3

Returns:
    The received message or None if the response timed out

methods.name: hivemind_bus_client.protocol.HiveMindSlaveInternalProtocol.handle_send
documentation:
ovos wants to send a HiveMessage

a device can be both a master and a slave, upstream messages are handled here

HiveMindListenerInternalProtocol will handle requests meant to go downstream
mycroft handlers  - from slave -> master
